text:
On a moonlit night, two mice were playing at the edge of Mrs. Butterworth's kitchen. They were giggling and diving into her endless supply of buttercream cookies.

Mrs. Butterworth was startled awake by their chittering. She peered out the window, her fur standing on end. The little mice had snuck back into the house. She crept softly to her cookie jar and scooped out a fluffy handful. Then she dangled it in front of them.

"Would you like a taste?" she asked sweetly.

One mouse blinked, then cautiously nibbled. It scooped up a bite. Mrs. Butterworth laughed quietly. They nibbled in a playful dance, chocolate filling disappearing into night.

Later, under the warmth of her quilt, they dreamed of sugar rushes and moonlit frosted tails. In their dreams, they were master pastry thieves. But when morning dawned, the biggest mouse hopped away, leaving the others to snooze with cookies
